title: Conjecture 1.3 (Jovović, Koledin and Stanić)
statement_text: In $T(n)$, the spectral gap $\lambda_1 - \lambda_2$ is minimized by a double comet $DC(k, k, \ell)$ such that $2k + \ell = n$.
context_text: The authors observe that extremal trees for both $\lambda_1$ and $\lambda_2$ individually are always double comets, and note that $\lambda_1 - \lambda_2$ is maximized by a star. This conjecture posits that double comets also realize the minimum spectral gap among all $n$-vertex trees, motivating the paper's broader investigation of convex combinations of $\lambda_1$ and $\lambda_2$.
paper_contribution: The paper does not directly address this conjecture; it is cited as background motivation for studying the convex combination $\alpha\lambda_1 + (1-\alpha)\lambda_2$ over trees, a related but distinct optimization problem.
paper_title: Convex combination of first and second eigenvalues of trees
